title: Vote packets omit the frozen document, rubric, and verification evidence

evidence.quote: Post-review verification evidence has a separate hash and is included in every vote packet.

return fmt.Sprintf("%s

PACKET HASH: %s
VOTER ID TO EMIT: %s

ANONYMOUS FINDINGS (untrusted model output):
%s", untrustedNotice, packet.PacketHash, reviewer.ID, payload)

evidence.note: Vote delivery records finding IDs and a blind finding packet, not packet items, chunks, rubric, or verification evidence.

observed: The voter receives only packet hash, voter ID, and anonymous finding objects; verification evidence is collected earlier but is not passed into votePrompt or the blind vote artifact.
expected_boundary: Every voter receives the same frozen document packet, rubric, pre-review evidence, and separately hashed post-review evidence while peer identity remains blinded.
failure_mechanism: The review-to-vote orchestration drops adjudication context, so votes can rely only on model-authored finding prose and quotes.
break_it_angle: Submit a plausible finding whose quote is contradicted elsewhere in the packet; the voter has no supplied packet content with which to detect the contradiction.
impact: Consensus, median severity, dissent, and arbitration are not independently grounded in the reviewed document.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Define a versioned vote packet containing packet items/chunks, rubric, pre-review evidence, verification evidence/hash, and blinded findings; persist the exact delivery per voter.
mitigation.behavior_test: Assert byte/hash-equivalent packet, rubric, and verification delivery for every voter and no reviewer/persona identity leakage.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The repair spans prompt contracts, orchestration, delivery artifacts, schemas, and end-to-end tests.

validation: Vote adapter golden includes packet items/chunks, rubric hash, verification hash, and evidence IDs.
Contradicted finding can be rejected using only the supplied vote packet.
non_goals: Do not reveal reviewer or persona identity in the blind packet.
review_limits: Static source audit; no live model vote was executed.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The declared requirement and omission in prompt/orchestration are both directly quoted.

skill_escalation.reason: Missing context weakens manipulated-content and output-integrity controls.

title: Transport success is promoted to semantic evidence verification

evidence.note: Fetch validates HTTP status, byte cap, provenance hash, and excerpt only; it does not evaluate support for the finding.
A worker-verified factual claim bypasses the unverified-claim path.

observed: Any successfully fetched allowlisted reference marks the finding worker-verified, regardless of whether the content supports, contradicts, or is unrelated to the claim.
expected_boundary: Retrieval provenance and semantic support are separate states; only a typed supports verdict bound to the claim and evidence may raise evidence authority.
failure_mechanism: Model-selected evidence crosses from untrusted citation text to authoritative verification through a transport-only worker.
break_it_angle: Attach an unrelated allowlisted 200 URL or a real DOI that contradicts the finding; the current path still marks the finding worker-verified.
impact: Irrelevant or fabricated citations can bypass the factual-claim evidence gate and influence accepted recommendations.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Persist retrieved separately from supports/contradicts/indeterminate, bind each verification task to normalized claim and finding IDs, and promote only a validated supports verdict.
mitigation.behavior_test: Unrelated and contradictory 200 responses remain unverified; only a supporting source becomes worker-verified.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Worker contracts, evidence state, vote packets, and adversarial citation fixtures must change together.

validation: Test unrelated, contradictory, supporting, failed, and search-result-page evidence fixtures.
Test fetched-content injection remains fenced and cannot set its own verdict.
non_goals: Do not weaken the existing SSRF and response-bound controls.
review_limits: No network request or model verifier was executed.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The authority promotion is a deterministic source path.

skill_escalation.reason: The defect is a lower-to-higher trust-boundary inversion.

title: Resume reruns and overwrites instead of continuing a checkpoint

evidence.quote: Resume reacquires locks, validates schema/path/hash checkpoints, and continues the first incomplete idempotent step.
return s.Review(ctx, ReviewOptions{Packet: &packet, PanelValue: &meta.Panel, RunID: runID, RunDir: runDir, Workspace: &workspace, ReplayOf: meta.ReplayOf})

evidence.note: Review rewrites start artifacts and begins the complete pass-1 workflow.
The incomplete-resume test checks only eventual completion, not provider-call duplication or artifact preservation.

observed: An incomplete run is resumed by invoking the entire Review use case with the same run directory, replacing same-path artifacts and repeating provider work.
expected_boundary: Validate journal/state/hash checkpoints and continue exactly the first incomplete idempotent phase while preserving completed invocation evidence.
failure_mechanism: Recovery is a full workflow restart rather than a checkpoint reducer with phase handlers.
break_it_angle: Interrupt after pass-1 persistence and resume; completed reviewers are called again and their call artifacts are written at the same paths.
impact: Duplicate provider cost, changed outcomes under one run identity, and loss of forensic evidence.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Reduce journal/state and validated artifacts to the first incomplete phase, use immutable invocation attempt IDs, and refuse/quarantine inconsistent checkpoints.
mitigation.behavior_test: Kill/restart at every phase and assert zero duplicate calls plus unchanged hashes for completed artifacts.

implementation_assessment.rationale: A phase protocol and comprehensive fault-injection suite are required across app and storage.

validation: Adapter invocation counts do not increase for completed phases after resume.
Completed call artifact hashes and timestamps remain unchanged.
Resume continues from the first absent validated artifact.
non_goals: Do not convert replay into resume; replay intentionally creates a new run.
review_limits: No process crash was injected; runtime crash manifestation remains unobserved.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The full Review re-entry and same-path writes are deterministic source properties.

skill_escalation.reason: A repair should deeply model interruption points and duplicate effects.

failure_analysis.failure_mode: full paid workflow rerun under the same run identity
failure_analysis.likely_cause: resume delegates to Review instead of phase handlers
failure_analysis.system_or_operator_effect: run appears resumed but no longer represents one immutable deliberation

single_point_failure.required_owner_decision: Define the canonical checkpoint recovery protocol.

title: Persisted packet identity is not cryptographically revalidated

evidence.note: Resume and replay compare meta.PacketHash only to the unverified packet.PacketHash field.
The specification declares packet content and paths untrusted and binds packet identity to canonical SHA-256.

observed: Persisted packet content, item hashes, rubric/evidence hashes, canonical manifest, and paths are not recomputed or semantically validated on read.
expected_boundary: Every trust re-entry must strictly validate the full packet object graph, recompute canonical hashes, and cross-check packet/meta/manifest/state identity before side effects.
failure_mechanism: Stored hash fields are treated as proof of the content they describe.
break_it_angle: Modify persisted packet content while retaining its old packet_hash; the packet reader and meta comparison accept it.
impact: Corrupt or same-user-tampered state can be resumed, replayed, or used for edit proposals under false provenance.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Centralize persisted-packet validation, recompute canonical/item/rubric/evidence hashes, cross-check all identity artifacts, and quarantine mismatches.
mitigation.behavior_test: Mutate every persisted packet field while preserving old identity fields; resume, replay, and edit must refuse before calls or writes.

implementation_assessment.rationale: A central validator is bounded, but all packet consumers and corruption fixtures must adopt it.

validation: Packet/meta/manifest/state mismatch matrix fails closed.
No provider call or source write occurs after a failed packet validation.
review_limits: Local state tampering was reasoned from source and not executed.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The reader's checks are directly visible and omit recomputation.

skill_escalation.reason: Repair should validate identity across packet, manifest, state, and edit consumers.

title: Edit and revert mutations are not transactionally coupled to lifecycle records

evidence.note: Backups and source files are written before edit-record.json; only ordinary returned errors invoke rollback.
Revert writes source originals before RevertedAt and final publication.

observed: A lifecycle/publication error after apply returns aborted while edited documents remain. Process-death windows can occur after some document writes but before a durable edit record; revert has a symmetric window.
expected_boundary: A prepared durable transaction must precede mutation and recovery must deterministically commit, roll back, or enter manual hold with accurate state.
failure_mechanism: Per-file atomic writes are not a workflow transaction across backups, multiple source files, journal/state, edit record, and final projections.
break_it_angle: Inject failure after each source replacement and before edit-record/transition/final writes; then restart edit, revert, and resume.
impact: User documents can diverge from Tribunal's durable status, leaving revert unavailable or retry safety ambiguous.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Journal a prepared edit transaction with all hashes and per-file progress before mutation; commit lifecycle projections or reconcile/rollback on recovery.
mitigation.behavior_test: Fault-inject every durable boundary and assert all-old, all-new, or explicit recoverable manual-hold state.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Multi-file crash consistency must be coordinated with lifecycle, publication, and revert semantics.

validation: Fault injection after each backup/source/record/state/final write.
Restart reconciles the exact transaction without overwriting later user changes.
review_limits: No process was killed mid-edit; confidence is capped at Likely for crash manifestation.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Missing transaction markers and deterministic post-apply error paths are visible; crash residue was not reproduced.

skill_escalation.reason: A repair needs interruption-point and duplicate-effect modeling.

failure_analysis.failure_mode: document mutation outlives failed or absent lifecycle commit
failure_analysis.likely_cause: file atomicity substituted for workflow atomicity
failure_analysis.system_or_operator_effect: user sees aborted command with changed document
failure_analysis.compensating_provision: Backups exist when the process reaches backup creation.

single_point_failure.required_owner_decision: Choose commit-vs-rollback recovery semantics for interrupted multi-file edits.

title: Token budget is a preflight estimate rather than a runtime circuit breaker

evidence.quote: All loops, calls, outputs, findings, verification tasks, arbitration items, time, and token use are capped.

evidence.note: OpenAI usage is parsed into Response, but orchestration does not accumulate or enforce it.

observed: One byte-based estimate runs before review and excludes framing, output, votes, verification, and retries; actual response usage is discarded.
expected_boundary: Reserve and reconcile per-call usage against a durable per-run token/cost ledger and stop before the next call would exceed the ceiling.
failure_mechanism: A heuristic preflight is presented as a runtime cap, leaving retries and later phases without a circuit breaker.
break_it_angle: Use near-limit input plus contract retries and maximal outputs; subsequent calls are not blocked by consumed usage.
impact: A run can exceed the operator's token or cost ceiling while reporting normal progress.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Add a central durable budget ledger at provider invocation, reserve maximum next-call usage, reconcile reported usage, and fail visible when usage is absent or exhausted.
mitigation.behavior_test: No call starts when consumed plus reserved tokens/cost would exceed the configured ceiling.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The central invocation boundary is clear, but all adapters, retries, persistence, and status output need coverage.

validation: Usage accounting covers review, vote, edit, arbitration, and retries.
Missing provider usage fails according to an explicit conservative policy.
review_limits: No provider cost or token overrun was observed.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The absence of runtime accounting is directly visible.

title: Unevidenced severity cap is applied only to factual claims

evidence.quote: Unevidenced findings are capped at minor unless evidence attaches.

observed: Unevidenced findings in every category except factual-claim can retain major or blocker severity after acceptance.
expected_boundary: Apply the general evidence cap to all categories before category-specific consensus overlays.
failure_mechanism: The global severity invariant was implemented inside the factual-claim special case.
break_it_angle: Accept an unevidenced correctness or integrity finding with blocker severity; the decision retains blocker.
impact: Unsupported findings can drive blocking exit status and be presented as major recommendations.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Cap severity for every unevidenced finding before category-specific outcome rules.
mitigation.behavior_test: Table-test every category, evidence status, and severity combination.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The domain rule is localized and can be locked by exhaustive table tests.

validation: All unevidenced categories cap at minor.
Anchored and genuinely worker-verified findings retain median severity subject to strict rules.
review_limits: Static rule review; no CLI run executed.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The specification and branch predicate directly contradict.

title: Persisted readers accept unknown nested schema versions

evidence.quote: Every persisted JSON object carries schema_version; missing and unknown versions fail closed.

evidence.note: readFinal validates only the outer final version and identity.
LoadLedger validates only the outer ledger version.

observed: Durable readers use permissive unmarshal and outer-only version checks; nested findings, evidence, votes, clusters, decisions, and disputes can carry missing or unknown versions.
expected_boundary: Each artifact reader must dispatch a complete strict schema and reject missing/unknown nested versions, unknown fields, and semantic identity mismatches.
failure_mechanism: Write-time Go types and provider schemas are incorrectly treated as durable read-time validation.
break_it_angle: Change a nested finding or decision schema_version while leaving final.schema_version at 1; readFinal accepts it.
impact: Corrupt or future-format data silently acquires current semantics in replay, explain, edit, and ledger paths.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Create strict per-artifact readers with version dispatch for the complete object graph.
mitigation.behavior_test: Unknown/missing nested versions and unknown fields fail with artifact-path context.

implementation_assessment.rationale: All persisted artifact types and reader call sites require a coordinated schema matrix.

validation: Golden reader matrix for every artifact and nested type.
Future schema versions fail closed without field-presence inference.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Reader implementation and outer-only checks are directly quoted.

title: Workspace ledger advances before terminal publication succeeds

evidence.quote: Terminal success requires durable terminal state and final.json.

observed: The cross-run findings ledger is updated before reports, terminal state/final, latest, and active projections; later failure leaves the ledger authoritative with no successful terminal publication.
expected_boundary: Workspace projections advance only from an immutable committed terminal run, or from an explicit recoverable publication transaction.
failure_mechanism: A multi-artifact publication sequence writes its broadest authoritative projection first and has no commit marker or compensation.
break_it_angle: Fault report or final write after UpdateLedger; findings list changes although the run remains incomplete.
impact: Status/resume and findings views can disagree about whether decisions are terminal; retry lacks an auditable commit boundary.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Commit terminal run artifacts first, then idempotently derive workspace projections under a publication transaction marker.
mitigation.behavior_test: Fault each publication write and assert no uncommitted ledger change or a visible recoverable pending-publication state.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Publication ordering is localized but recovery and projection repair require fault tests.

validation: Publication fault matrix covers reports, final, latest, and active.
Projection replay from committed terminal artifact is idempotent.
review_limits: No filesystem write failure was injected.

confidence_calibration.rationale: The deterministic partial-write ordering is directly quoted.

failure_analysis.failure_mode: ledger projection commits before terminal source record
failure_analysis.likely_cause: independent atomic files without a workflow commit point
failure_analysis.system_or_operator_effect: operator treats incomplete run findings as authoritative
failure_analysis.compensating_provision: publish lock prevents concurrent publishers but not partial publication

title: Release archive smoke occurs after publication

evidence.quote:
publish:
  name: Publish signed release
  needs: verify
...
          args: release --clean

archive-smoke:
  name: Verify archive (${{ matrix.goos }}/${{ matrix.goarch }})
  needs: publish
...
        run: gh release download "${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"

observed: The release is published before target-platform archives are downloaded and verify-install is run.
expected_boundary: Smoke the exact immutable archive bytes on all target platforms before external release publication and attestation.
failure_mechanism: Artifact production and publication are combined, forcing acceptance tests to run after the external side effect.
break_it_angle: Produce an archive with a packaging/layout error; archive-smoke fails only after users can download it.
impact: Known-bad assets may be publicly available and require manual cleanup; attestations can precede installation acceptance.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Build/upload pre-release artifacts, smoke exact checksums on the matrix, then publish and attest only after all jobs pass.
mitigation.behavior_test: Malformed archive fails before any GitHub Release asset or attestation exists.

implementation_assessment.rationale: The workflow must separate build, artifact transfer, platform smoke, and publish permissions.

validation: Failed archive smoke leaves no public release assets.
Published checksums equal the artifacts tested on every platform.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Job dependencies and publication command are explicit.

title: No machine-evaluable architecture intent or invariant registry

evidence.note: Repository search found no .architecture directory, invariant registry, exception file, intent map, or baseline.

evidence.quote: The domain imports only Go standard-library value types. Interfaces parse raw input, application code owns orchestration and barriers, and adapters translate filesystem/network/process details.

evidence.note: Binding invariants exist only as prose declarations.

observed: Architectural intent is documented in prose but has no stable machine-evaluable nodes, owners, assertions, exceptions, or baseline.
expected_boundary: A versioned intent/invariant registry should bind accepted SPEC/ADR constraints to owned scopes, evidence-producing checks, explicit exceptions, and a baseline.
failure_mechanism: Documentation is the only conformance mechanism, so drift has no stable identity, CI gate, or trend signal.
break_it_angle: Change a vote-packet or lifecycle boundary while keeping tests green; no architecture invariant independently rejects the contradiction.
impact: Repeated drift, ownership ambiguity, and no defensible architecture health/trend or new-vs-known gating.

mitigation.minimal_repair: Use plan-architecture-invariants to define a registry and baseline seeded from accepted SPEC/ADR constraints and regression tests.
mitigation.behavior_test: A deterministic invariant fixture fails CI for a packet/vote/lifecycle dependency violation.

implementation_assessment.rationale: Maintainers must own the intended rules and exception policy; tooling can then encode them.

validation: Registry validates and every enabled invariant has one disposition.
Exceptions require an owner and expiry.
One deterministic invariant is recomputed manually from raw evidence.
non_goals: This audit does not define or modify the repository's intended architecture.
review_limits: Bootstrap mode; inferred-intent findings are capped at Likely and trend is unavailable.

confidence_calibration.rationale: Registry absence is source-evidenced, but the necessity and intended ownership model are architecture-governance judgments.

skill_escalation.reason: The planner owns intent definition, invariant authoring, and baseline creation.
